title: <div><tt>| `_`    | Soft separator | Internal threading, nested logic |</tt></di
content:
| `_` | Soft separator | Internal threading, nested logic |
| CAPS | Ritual/Object | System commands, folders, or outputs |
| PascalCase | Identity/Form | Projects, Personas, Outputs |
| lowercase | Logic/file | Raw files, scripts, utility modules |
| `-` | Export Readability | External file clarity (for Git, public docs) |
| `/` | Banned | Causes system conflict across devices/files |

---

## CANONICAL VS STYLED

All major elements (artist IDs, projects, outputs) exist in two states:

1. **Canonical** (system-safe)
- All lowercase
- Used for file paths, sync, and automation
- e.g. `etceter4`, `met4morfoses`, `grinder`

2. **Styled** (expressive identity)
- LeetSpeak, case-sensitive, symbolic
- Used for user-facing documents, visuals, and exports
